fields.name: No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of the involved entry or record, according to the specified number series.

fields.name: Name
fields.tooltip: Specifies the vendor's name.

fields.name: Search Name
fields.tooltip: Specifies an alternate name that you can use to search for the record in question when you cannot remember the value in the Name field.

fields.name: Name 2
fields.tooltip: Specifies an additional part of the name.

fields.name: Address
fields.tooltip: Specifies the vendor's address.

fields.name: Address 2
fields.tooltip: Specifies additional address information.

fields.name: City
fields.tooltip: Specifies the vendor's city.

fields.name: Contact
fields.tooltip: Specifies the name of the person you regularly contact when you do business with this vendor.

fields.name: Phone No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the phone number.

fields.name: Telex No.
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the vendor's telex number. You can enter a maximum of 20 characters, both numbers and letters.
Use a standard format to enter the telex number so that it will look uniform on printouts.

fields.name: Our Account No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies your account number with the vendor, if you have one.

fields.name: Territory Code
fields.tooltip:
Specifies a territory code for the vendor. To see the territory codes in the Territory table, click the field.
You can use this field to group vendors by territory in order to make purchasing for different inventory locations more efficient. For example, choose the code SCAND in the Territory Code field for all vendors from Scandinavia. In the future, when you are purchasing for the warehouse in Norway, you can set a filter so the program shows only Scandinavian vendors. This makes it easy to see the vendors you already have in Scandinavia.

fields.name: Global Dimension 1 Code
fields.tooltip:
Specifies a dimension value code for a dimension. To see the dimension value codes you have set up for this dimension in the Dimension Values window, click the field.
If you enter a dimension value code in this field, the program will use the corresponding dimension value as default each time you enter the account on an entry line. Default dimension values are only suggestions and you can change them on the entry line, for example in a journal.
Click here to learn more about dimensions.

fields.name: Global Dimension 2 Code
fields.tooltip:
Specifies a dimension value code for a dimension. To see the dimension value codes you have set up for this dimension in the Dimension Values window, click the field.
If you enter a dimension value code in this field, the program will use the corresponding dimension value as default each time you enter the account on an entry line. Default dimension values are only suggestions and you can change them on the entry line, for example in a journal.
Click here to learn more about dimensions.

fields.name: Budgeted Amount
fields.tooltip:
Specifies a budget amount for the vendor.
You cannot set up budgets for vendors in the same way that you can for G/L accounts. Instead, you can enter one budget figure per vendor in this field. This can represent, for example, the expected purchases from this vendor.
This field has no relationship to possible budgets in the general ledger.

fields.name: Vendor Posting Group
fields.tooltip: Specifies the vendor's market type to link business transactions made for the vendor with the appropriate account in the general ledger.

fields.name: Currency Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the currency code that is inserted by default when you create purchase documents or journal lines for the vendor.

fields.name: Language Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the language that is used when translating specified text on documents to foreign business partner, such as an item description on an order confirmation.

fields.name: Registration Number
fields.tooltip: Specifies the vendor's registration number.

fields.name: Statistics Group
fields.tooltip: Specifies the statistics group.

fields.name: Payment Terms Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies a formula that calculates the payment due date, payment discount date, and payment discount amount.

fields.name: Fin. Charge Terms Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the code for the involved finance charges in case of late payment.

fields.name: Purchaser Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies which purchaser is assigned to the vendor.

fields.name: Shipment Method Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the delivery conditions of the related shipment, such as free on board (FOB).

fields.name: Shipping Agent Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the shipping agent that the vendor normally uses for your orders. You can enter a maximum of 20 characters, both numbers and letters.

fields.name: Invoice Disc. Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the vendor's invoice discount code. When you set up a new vendor card, the number you have entered in the No. field is automatically inserted.

fields.name: Country/Region Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the country/region of the address.

fields.name: Comment
fields.tooltip:
Specifies whether a comment has been entered for this vendor.
The comment could be about a particular shipment method or payment problems, for example..
To see or set up comments, click Related Information, point to Vendor, and then choose Comment. See also the Comment Line table.
You can also enter a comment in Notes FactBox.

fields.name: Blocked
fields.tooltip: Specifies whether transactions with this vendor are restricted. Use this field to control vendor activity, such as temporarily halting payments or fully blocking a vendor due to compliance or business decisions. The field can have one of the following values: (blank) - No restrictions, all transactions are allowed. All - All transactions with this vendor are blocked. You cannot create or post any documents or journals with the vendor. Payment - Only payment transactions are blocked. You can still create and post purchase documents, but you cannot make payments to the vendor.

fields.name: Pay-to Vendor No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the vendor's pay-to vendor (who should receive invoices).

fields.name: Priority
fields.tooltip: Specifies the importance of the vendor when suggesting payments using the Suggest Vendor Payments function.

fields.name: Payment Method Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies how to make payment, such as with bank transfer, cash, or check.

fields.name: Format Region
fields.tooltip: Specifies the region format to be used on printouts for this vendor.

fields.name: First Transaction Date
fields.tooltip: Specifies the date of the first transaction with the vendor.

fields.name: Last Date Modified
fields.tooltip: Specifies when the vendor card was last modified.

fields.name: Date Filter
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the date interval that values are filtered by.
If this field contains the starting and ending dates for a date interval, then other field values only show information for that interval. For example, the Net Change field shows the net change for that interval only.
You can enter a date interval according to the following rules.


Meaning


Sample expression


Entries that are included


Equal to

12 15 12

Only entries that are posted on December 15, 2012.


Interval

12 15 12..01 15 13
..12 15 12

Entries that are posted on dates between and including December 15, 2012, and January 15, 2013.
Entries that are posted on January 15, 2012, or earlier.


Either/or

12 15 12|12 16 12

Entries that are posted on either December 15, 2012, or December 16, 2012. If there are entries that are posted on both days, then all entries are displayed.


You can also combine the various format types.


Sample expression


Entries that are included


12 15 12|12 01 12..12 10 12

Entries that are posted either on December 15, 2012, or on dates between and including December 1, 2012, and December 10, 2012.


..12 14 12|12 30 12..

Entries that are posted on December 14, 2012, or earlier, or entries that are posted on December 30, 2012, or later, which includes all entries except those that are posted on dates between and including December 15, 2012, and December 29, 2012.

fields.name: Global Dimension 1 Filter
fields.tooltip:
Specifies a filter for a dimension, so that fields such as Balance (LCY) will display the balance based only on the dimension values included in the filter.
Click here to learn more about dimensions.

fields.name: Global Dimension 2 Filter
fields.tooltip:
Specifies a filter for a dimension, so that fields such as Balance (LCY) will display the balance based only on the dimension values included in the filter.
Click here to learn more about dimensions.

fields.name: Balance
fields.tooltip:
Shows the vendor's current balance. If you invoice a vendor in more than one currency, this balance will be the sum of entries in different currencies. In this case, you will need to filter the entries that make up the balance by using a currency filter.
You can filter the Balance field so that its contents are calculated only on the basis of certain Global Dimension 1 values, Global Dimension 2 values and/or currencies.
The program uses the Amount field in the Vendor Ledger Entry table to calculate and update the contents of this field.
To see the vendor ledger entries that make up the amount shown, click the field.

fields.name: Balance (LCY)
fields.tooltip: Specifies the total value of your completed purchases from the vendor in the current fiscal year. It is calculated from amounts including VAT on all completed purchase invoices and credit memos.

fields.name: Net Change
fields.tooltip:
Shows the net change in the vendor's account for the period entered in the Date Filter field. If you invoice a vendor in more than one currency, this amount will include different currencies. In this case, you will need to filter the amount by using a currency filter.
You can filter the Net Change field so its contents are calculated only on the basis of certain Global Dimension 1 values, Global Dimension 2 values, currencies and/or dates.
The program uses the Amount field in the Vendor Ledger Entry table to calculate and update the contents of this field.
To see the vendor ledger entries that make up the amount shown, click the field.

fields.name: Net Change (LCY)
fields.tooltip:
Shows the net change in the vendor's account for the period entered in the Date Filter field in LCY.
The program uses the Amount (LCY) field in the Vendor Ledger Entry table to calculate and regularly update the contents of this field.
You can filter the Net Change (LCY) field so its contents are calculated only on the basis of certain Global Dimension 1 values, Global Dimension 2 values and/or dates.
To see the vendor ledger entries that make up the amount shown, click the field.

fields.name: Purchases (LCY)
fields.tooltip:
Shows the total amount the company has purchased from this vendor in LCY.
The program uses the Purchase (LCY) field in the Vendor Ledger Entry table to calculate and regularly update the contents of this field.
You can filter the Purchase (LCY) field so its contents are calculated only on the basis of certain Global Dimension 1 values, Global Dimension 2 values and/or dates.
To see the vendor ledger entries that make up the amount shown, click the field.

fields.name: Inv. Discounts (LCY)
fields.tooltip:
Shows the total invoice discount obtained from this vendor in LCY.
The program uses the Inv. Discount (LCY) field in the Vendor Ledger Entry table to calculate and regularly update the contents of this field.
You can filter the Inv. Discounts (LCY) field so its contents are calculated only on the basis of certain Global Dimension 1 values, Global Dimension 2 values, and/or dates.
To see the vendor ledger entries that make up the amount shown, click the field.

fields.name: Pmt. Discounts (LCY)
fields.tooltip:
Shows the total amount of payment discounts obtained from this vendor in LCY.
The program uses the Pmt. Disc. Rcd.(LCY) field in the Vendor Ledger Entry table to calculate and regularly update the contents of this field.
You can filter the Pmt. Discounts (LCY) field so its contents are calculated only on the basis of certain Global Dimension 1, Global Dimension 2 values, and/or dates.
To see the vendor ledger entries that make up the amount shown, click the field.

fields.name: Balance Due
fields.tooltip:
Shows the balance due to this vendor. If you invoice a vendor in more than one currency, this amount will include different currencies. In this case, you will need to filter the amount by using a currency filter.
You can filter the Balance Due field so its contents are calculated only on the basis of certain Global Dimension 1 values, Global Dimension 2 values, and/or currencies and dates.
The program uses the Remaining Amount field in the Vendor Ledger Entry table to calculate and update the contents of this field.
To see the vendor ledger entries that make up the amount shown, click the field.

fields.name: Balance Due (LCY)
fields.tooltip: Specifies the total value of your unpaid purchases from the vendor in the current fiscal year. It is calculated from amounts including VAT on all open purchase invoices and credit memos.

fields.name: Payments
fields.tooltip:
Shows the total amount that has been paid to this vendor. If you invoice a vendor in more than one currency, this amount will include different currencies. In this case, you will need to filter the amount by using a currency filter.
You can filter the Payments field so its contents are calculated only on the basis of certain Global Dimension 1 values, Global Dimension 2 values, currencies and/or dates.
The program uses the Amount field for those entries in the Vendor Ledger Entry table where the document type is Payment to calculate and update the contents of this field.
To see the vendor ledger entries that make up the amount shown, click the field.

fields.name: Invoice Amounts
fields.tooltip:
Shows the total amount you have been invoiced for by this vendor. If you invoice a vendor in more than one currency, this amount will include different currencies. In this case, you will need to filter the amount by using a currency filter.
You can filter the Invoice Amounts field so its contents are calculated only on the basis of certain Global Dimension 1 values, Global Dimension 2 values, currencies and/or dates.
The program uses the Amount field for those entries in the Vendor Ledger Entry table where the document type is Invoice to calculate and update the contents of this field.
To see the vendor ledger entries that make up the amount shown, click the field.

fields.name: Cr. Memo Amounts
fields.tooltip:
Shows the total amount of the credit memos issued by this vendor. If you invoice a vendor in more than one currency, this amount will include different currencies. In this case, you will need to filter the amount by using a currency filter.
You can filter the Cr. Memo Amounts field so its contents are calculated only on the basis of certain Global Dimension 1 values, Global Dimension 2 values, currencies and/or dates.
The program uses the Amount field for those entries in the Vendor Ledger Entry table where the document type is Credit Memo to calculate and update the contents of this field.
To see the vendor ledger entries that make up the amount shown, click the field.

fields.name: Finance Charge Memo Amounts
fields.tooltip:
Shows the total amount this vendor has added in finance charges. If you invoice a vendor in more than one currency, this amount will include different currencies. In this case, you will need to filter the amount by using a currency filter.
You can filter the Finance Charge Memo Amounts field so that its contents are calculated only on the basis of certain Global Dimension 1 values, Global Dimension 2 values, currencies and/or dates.
The program calculates and updates the contents of this field using the Amount field for those entries in the Vendor Ledger Entry table where the document type is Finance Charge Memo.
To see the vendor ledger entries that make up the amount shown, click the field.

fields.name: Payments (LCY)
fields.tooltip: Specifies the sum of payments paid to the vendor.

fields.name: Inv. Amounts (LCY)
fields.tooltip:
Shows the total amount you have been invoiced by this vendor in LCY.
The program uses the Original Amt. (LCY) field for those entries in the Vendor Ledger Entry table where the document type is Invoice to calculate and regularly update the contents of this field.
You can filter the Inv. Amounts (LCY) field so its contents are calculated only on the basis of certain Global Dimension 1 values, Global Dimension 2 values and/or dates.
To see the vendor ledger entries that make up the amount shown, click the field.

fields.name: Cr. Memo Amounts (LCY)
fields.tooltip:
Shows the total amount of credit memos issued by this vendor in LCY.
The program uses the Original Amt. (LCY) field for those entries in the Vendor Ledger Entry table where the document type is Credit Memo to calculate and regularly update the contents of this field.
You can filter the Cr. Memo Amounts (LCY) field so its contents are calculated only on the basis of certain Global Dimension 1 values, Global Dimension 2 values and/or dates.
To see the vendor ledger entries that make up the amount shown, click the field.

fields.name: Fin. Charge Memo Amounts (LCY)
fields.tooltip:
Shows the total amount this vendor has added in finance charges in LCY.
The program calculates and updates the contents of this field using the Original Amt. (LCY) field for those entries in the Vendor Ledger Entry table where the document type is Finance Charge Memo.
You can filter the Fin. Charge Memo Amounts (LCY) field so that its contents are calculated only on the basis of certain Global Dimension 1 values, Global Dimension 2 values and/or dates.
To see the vendor ledger entries that make up the amount shown, click the field.

fields.name: Outstanding Orders
fields.tooltip:
Shows the sum of outstanding orders to this vendor. If you invoice a vendor in more than one currency, this amount will include different currencies. In this case, you will need to filter the amount by using a currency filter.
You can filter the Outstanding Orders field so its contents are calculated only on the basis of certain Global Dimension 1 values, Global Dimension 2 values, currencies and/or dates.
The program uses the Outstanding Amount field for those entries in the Purchase Line table where the document type is Order to calculate and update the contents of this field.
To see the vendor ledger entries that make up the amount shown, click the field.

fields.name: Amt. Rcd. Not Invoiced
fields.tooltip:
Shows the total invoice amount of items you have received but not yet been invoiced for. If you invoice a vendor in more than one currency, this amount will include different currencies. In this case, you will need to filter the amount by using a currency filter.
You can filter the Amt. Rcd. Not Invoiced field so its contents are calculated only on the basis of certain Global Dimension 1 values, Global Dimension 2 values, currencies and/or dates.
The program uses the Amt. Rcd. Not Invoiced field for those entries in the Purchase Line table where the document type is Order to calculate and update the contents of this field.
To see the vendor ledger entries that make up the amount shown, click the field.

fields.name: Application Method
fields.tooltip: Specifies how to apply payments to entries for this vendor.

fields.name: Prices Including VAT
fields.tooltip: Specifies if the Unit Price and Line Amount fields on document lines should be shown with or without VAT.

fields.name: Fax No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the vendor's fax number.

fields.name: Telex Answer Back
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the vendor's telex answer back code. You can enter a maximum of 20 characters, both numbers and letters.
Use a standard format to enter the telex code, so it will look uniform on printouts.

fields.name: VAT Registration No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the vendor's VAT registration number.

fields.name: Gen. Bus. Posting Group
fields.tooltip: Specifies the vendor's trade type to link transactions made for this vendor with the appropriate general ledger account according to the general posting setup.

fields.name: GLN
fields.tooltip: Specifies the vendor in connection with electronic document receiving.

fields.name: Post Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the post code.

fields.name: County
fields.tooltip: Specifies the state, province or county as a part of the address.

fields.name: EORI Number
fields.tooltip: Specifies the Economic Operators Registration and Identification number that is used when you exchange information with the customs authorities due to trade into or out of the European Union.

fields.name: Debit Amount
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the total of the debit entries that have been posted to the vendor's account. If you invoice a vendor in more than one currency, this amount will include different currencies. In this case, you will need to filter the amount by using a currency filter.
You can filter the Debit Amount field so that its contents are calculated only on the basis of certain Global Dimension 1 values, Global Dimension 2 values, currencies and/or dates.
To see the ledger entries that comprise the debit amount, click the field.

fields.name: Credit Amount
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the total of the credit entries that have been posted to the vendor's account. If you invoice a vendor in more than one currency, this amount will include different currencies. In this case, you will need to filter the amount by using a currency filter.
You can filter the Credit Amount field so that its contents are calculated only on the basis of certain Global Dimension 1 values, Global Dimension 2 values, currencies and/or dates.
To see the ledger entries that comprise the credit amount, click the field.

fields.name: Debit Amount (LCY)
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the total of the debit entries, in LCY, that have been posted to the vendor account.
To see the ledger entries that comprise the debit amount (in LCY), click the field.

fields.name: Credit Amount (LCY)
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the total of the credit entries, in LCY, that have been posted to the vendor account.
To see the ledger entries that comprise the credit amount (in LCY), click the field.

fields.name: E-Mail
fields.tooltip: Specifies the vendor's email address.

fields.name: Home Page
fields.tooltip: Specifies the vendor's web site.

fields.name: Reminder Amounts
fields.tooltip:
Shows the total amount of interest and additional fees that the vendor has added on reminders. If you invoice a vendor in more than one currency, this amount will include different currencies. In this case, you will need to filter the amount by using a currency filter.
You can filter the Reminder Amounts field so that its contents are based only on certain Global Dimension 1 values, Global Dimension 2 values, currencies and/or dates.
The program automatically calculates and updates the contents of the field using the Amount field for those entries in the Vendor Ledger Entry table where the document type is Reminder.
To see the vendor ledger entries that make up the amount shown, click the field.

fields.name: Reminder Amounts (LCY)
fields.tooltip:
Shows the total amount of interest and additional fees that the vendor has added on reminders in LCY.
The program automatically calculates and updates the contents of the field using those entries in the Original Amt. (LCY) field in the Vendor Ledger Entry table where the document type is Reminder.
You can filter the Reminder Amounts (LCY) field so that its contents are based only on certain Global Dimension 1 values, Global Dimension 2 values and/or dates.
To see the vendor ledger entries that make up the amount shown, click the field.

fields.name: No. Series
fields.tooltip: Specifies the code for the number series that was used to assign the vendor's number.

fields.name: Tax Area Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies a tax area code for the company.

fields.name: Tax Liable
fields.tooltip: Specifies if the customer is liable for sales tax.

fields.name: VAT Bus. Posting Group
fields.tooltip: Specifies the VAT specification of the involved customer or vendor to link transactions made for this record with the appropriate general ledger account according to the VAT posting setup.

fields.name: Currency Filter
fields.tooltip:
Specifies a currency filter so that the amounts in the Balance, Net Change, Balance Due and other FlowField fields contain entries in one or more currencies.
To see the currency codes in the Currencies window, click the field.

fields.name: Outstanding Orders (LCY)
fields.tooltip: Specifies the sum of outstanding orders (in LCY) to this vendor.

fields.name: Amt. Rcd. Not Invoiced (LCY)
fields.tooltip: Specifies the total invoice amount (in LCY) for the items you have received but not yet been invoiced for.

fields.name: Block Payment Tolerance
fields.tooltip: Specifies if the vendor allows payment tolerance.

fields.name: Pmt. Disc. Tolerance (LCY)
fields.tooltip: Specifies the payment amount in LCY that must be received as paid to allow the payment discount on payments that are past the payment discount date.

fields.name: Pmt. Tolerance (LCY)
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the amount of outstanding payment in LCY that is required to meet the payment tolerance conditions.

Additional Information

You can use payment tolerance so that every outstanding amount has a set maximum allowed payment tolerance. If the payment tolerance is met, then the payment amount is analyzed as follows:

If the payment amount is an underpayment, then the outstanding amount is fully closed by the underpayment. A detailed ledger entry is posted to the payment entry so that no remaining amount is left on the applied invoice entry.


If the payment amount is an overpayment, then a new detailed ledger entry is posted to the payment entry so that no remaining amount is left on the payment entry.

fields.name: IC Partner Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the vendor's IC partner code, if the vendor is one of your intercompany partners.

fields.name: Refunds
fields.tooltip: Specifies the sum of transactions with document type Refund.

fields.name: Refunds (LCY)
fields.tooltip: Specifies the sum of refunds paid to the vendor.

fields.name: Other Amounts
fields.tooltip: Specifies the sum of transactions where no document type is defined.

fields.name: Other Amounts (LCY)
fields.tooltip: Specifies the sum in LCY of transactions where no document type is defined.

fields.name: Prepayment %
fields.tooltip: Specifies a prepayment percentage that applies to all orders for this vendor, regardless of the items or services on the order lines.

fields.name: Outstanding Invoices
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the sum of the vendor's outstanding purchase invoices.
The sum is calculated and updated based on values in the Outstanding Amount field on purchase lines for the customer.

fields.name: Outstanding Invoices (LCY)
fields.tooltip: Specifies the sum of the vendor's outstanding purchase invoices in LCY.

fields.name: Pay-to No. Of Archived Doc.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of archived purchase orders that have been registered for the vendor when the vendor acts as the pay-to vendor.

fields.name: Buy-from No. Of Archived Doc.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of archived purchase orders that have been registered for the vendor acting as the Buy-from vendor.

fields.name: Partner Type
fields.tooltip: Specifies if the vendor is a person or a company.

fields.name: Intrastat Partner Type
fields.tooltip: Specifies for Intrastat reporting if the vendor is a person or a company.

fields.name: Exclude from Pmt. Practices
fields.tooltip: Specifies that the vendor must be excluded from Payment Practices calculations.

fields.name: Company Size Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the size of the vendor's company.

fields.name: Image
fields.tooltip: Specifies the picture of the vendor, for example, a logo.

fields.name: Privacy Blocked
fields.tooltip: Specifies whether to limit access to data for the data subject during daily operations. This is useful, for example, when protecting data from changes while it is under privacy review.

fields.name: Disable Search by Name
fields.tooltip: Specifies that you can change the vendor name on open purchase documents. The change applies only to the documents.

fields.name: Creditor No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of the vendor.

fields.name: Allow Multiple Posting Groups
fields.tooltip: Specifies if multiple posting groups can be used for posting business transactions for this customer.

fields.name: Self-Billing Agreement
fields.tooltip: Specifies the value of the Self-Billing Agreement field.

fields.name: Preferred Bank Account Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the vendor bank account that will be used by default on payment journal lines for export to a payment bank file.

fields.name: Coupled to Dataverse
fields.tooltip: Specifies that the vendor is coupled to an account in Dataverse.

fields.name: Cash Flow Payment Terms Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies a payment term that will be used for calculating cash flow.

fields.name: Primary Contact No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the primary contact number for the vendor.

fields.name: Mobile Phone No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the vendor's mobile telephone number.

fields.name: Responsibility Center
fields.tooltip: Specifies the code of the responsibility center, such as a distribution hub, that is associated with the involved user, company, customer, or vendor.

fields.name: Location Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the warehouse location where items from the vendor must be received by default.

fields.name: Lead Time Calculation
fields.tooltip: Specifies a date formula for the amount of time it takes to replenish the item.

fields.name: Price Calculation Method
fields.tooltip: Specifies the default price calculation method.

fields.name: No. of Pstd. Receipts
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of posted purchase receipts that exist for the vendor.

fields.name: No. of Pstd. Invoices
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of posted purchase invoices that exist for the vendor.

fields.name: No. of Pstd. Return Shipments
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of posted return shipments that exist for the vendor.

fields.name: No. of Pstd. Credit Memos
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of posted purchase credit memos that exist for the vendor.

fields.name: Pay-to No. of Orders
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of posted orders that exist for the vendor.

fields.name: Pay-to No. of Invoices
fields.tooltip: Specifies the amount that relates to invoices.

fields.name: Pay-to No. of Return Orders
fields.tooltip: Specifies how many return orders have been registered for the customer when the customer acts as the pay-to customer.

fields.name: Pay-to No. of Credit Memos
fields.tooltip: Specifies the amount that relates to credit memos.

fields.name: Pay-to No. of Pstd. Receipts
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of posted receipts that exist for the vendor.

fields.name: Pay-to No. of Pstd. Invoices
fields.tooltip: Specifies the amount that relates to posted invoices.

fields.name: Pay-to No. of Pstd. Return S.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of posted return shipments that exist for the vendor.

fields.name: Pay-to No. of Pstd. Cr. Memos
fields.tooltip: Specifies the amount that relates to credit memos.

fields.name: No. of Quotes
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of purchase quotes that exist for the vendor.

fields.name: No. of Blanket Orders
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of purchase blanket orders that exist for the vendor.

fields.name: No. of Orders
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of purchase orders that exist for the vendor.

fields.name: No. of Invoices
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of unposted purchase invoices that exist for the vendor.

fields.name: No. of Return Orders
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of purchase return orders that exist for the vendor.

fields.name: No. of Credit Memos
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of unposted purchase credit memos that exist for the vendor.

fields.name: No. of Order Addresses
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of order addresses that have been entered for this vendor.

fields.name: Pay-to No. of Quotes
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of quotes that exist for the vendor.

fields.name: Pay-to No. of Blanket Orders
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of blanket orders.

fields.name: No. of Incoming Documents
fields.tooltip: Specifies incoming documents, such as vendor invoices in PDF or as image files, that you can manually or automatically convert to document records, such as purchase invoices. The external files that represent incoming documents can be attached at any process stage, including to posted documents and to the resulting vendor, customer, and general ledger entries.

fields.name: Base Calendar Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies a customizable calendar for delivery planning that holds the vendor's working days and holidays.

fields.name: Document Sending Profile
fields.tooltip: Specifies the preferred method of sending documents to this vendor, so that you do not have to select a sending option every time that you post and send a document to the vendor. Documents to this vendor will be sent using the specified sending profile and will override the default document sending profile.

fields.name: Copy Buy-from Add. to Qte From
fields.tooltip: Specifies which vendor address is inserted on purchase quotes that you create for the vendor.

fields.name: Over-Receipt Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the policy that will be used for the vendor if more items than ordered are received.
